_comment_byte_weight: Summary-first guardrail for the homepage '/'. The homepage must render off data/summary.json and must NOT download the multi-MB raw offer shards on load. Measured total transfer for '/' against the local static build (npm run build + lhci autorun) is ~1,063,468 bytes (1,039 KiB). The total-byte-weight ceiling below is set to 1,400,000 bytes (~30% headroom) as an ERROR-level assertion: this is a STABLE regression signal — if '/' starts pulling raw shards on load, byte weight balloons well past this and CI fails. Far less noisy than the composite perf score.
_comment_performance: We lock in the CURRENT measured perf state to prevent regressions rather than chase an aspirational target. Locally (lhci vs the static build, with no third-party ads that the deployed site carries) '/' scores ~0.63. The composite perf score is run-to-run noisy, so the ERROR floor is set well below the measured value at 0.55 — enough to catch a real regression without flaking on variance.
